Despite a late surge from the Spurs, led by V. Wembanyama's dominant performance, it was not enough to overcome the Denver team. For the Spurs, V. Wembanyama led the way with an impressive 34-point game, including 18 rebounds and a plus/minus of +4. S. Champagnie (18 points).

In a closely contested game, the Nuggets ultimately took home the win with a score of 136-134 in regulation and overtime. The game flow is as follows: the Nuggets scored 36 points in the first quarter, while the Spurs responded with 43 points. The second quarter saw both teams score 29 points each, setting up a back-and-forth contest for the remainder of the game.

The top three Spurs by points were V. Wembanyama (34), S. Castle (20), and D. Vassell (18). V. Wembanyama's dominant performance was evident in his 34 points and 18 rebounds, while S. Castle contributed with 20 points and 9 assists.

The Spurs struggled to contain the Nuggets' defense, particularly in the fourth quarter where Denver outscored them 32-28.

report_text:
The Denver Nuggets beat the San Antonio Spurs 136-134 in a close game on Saturday, April 4th. The Spurs trailed by as many as 7 points at one point in the fourth quarter, but managed to tie the game with just seconds left before Denver's Jamal Murray hit a jump shot to seal the win.

The Nuggets outscored the Spurs in all four quarters, with their highest-scoring period coming in the second quarter when they scored 29 points. The Spurs' Vassell had the most points for his team, scoring 18, while teammate Wembanyama led Denver's offense with a dominant performance of 34 points and 18 rebounds.

The Nuggets got a strong all-around effort from Murray, who added 22 points to his already impressive night in the game. In a game that saw several players put up notable performances, V. Wembanyama led the Spurs with 34 points and 18 rebounds, while S. Castle contributed 20 points and 5 rebounds off the bench. On the other side of the court, the Nuggets' scoring top three were all from Denver's player ranks: V. Wembanyama was joined by teammate K. Olynyk in the top 3 as well—both players with no stats shown here but both tied at .0 ppg for the game. For Denver, J. Champagnie had 18 points and 5 rebounds, while D. Fox contributed 14 points.

In a closely contested affair that went down to the wire, the Nuggets ultimately emerged victorious behind their strong team play.
